title: Onboarding takes too long
description: New engineers take 3-4 weeks before they can ship their first PR. Documentation is scattered across Notion Confluence and tribal knowledge.

title: Deploy pipeline is flaky
description: CI/CD fails about 20% of the time with intermittent timeout errors. Teams retry and waste time waiting.

title: Customer feedback never reaches engineering
description: Product and support teams collect feedback but there is no systematic way it gets to the engineering team. We find out about pain points months later.

title: Too many meetings no decisions
description: We have 6+ recurring meetings per week but decisions are rarely documented or followed up on. People leave meetings unclear on next steps.

title: No visibility into what other teams are working on
description: Each squad works in isolation. We frequently discover duplicated effort or conflicting priorities too late.

title: Incident response is ad hoc
description: When production issues happen there is no runbook and no clear escalation path. The person who happens to notice the alert figures it out alone.

title: Knowledge lost when people leave
description: When someone leaves the company critical context about systems and decisions disappears. No handoff process exists.

title: Sprint planning is guesswork
description: We estimate stories based on gut feel. Velocity is unpredictable and we regularly miss sprint goals by 30-40%.

title: Support tickets pile up with no owner
description: Tier 2 support tickets that require engineering input sit in a queue with no clear triage process. Average response time is 5 days.

title: No shared understanding of technical debt
description: Everyone agrees we have tech debt but there is no inventory prioritization or dedicated time to address it. It keeps growing.
